name: Club of Heracles
description: The Club of Heracles is a legendary weapon, a massive and formidable club wielded by the hero Heracles (Hercules in Roman mythology). It is typically described as being made of olive wood, though some accounts suggest oak or ash, and was Heracles' primary weapon throughout many of his famous Twelve Labors and other heroic exploits. The club's immense size and weight allowed Heracles to deliver devastating blows, capable of crushing opponents, shattering stone, and felling trees. It was a symbol of his unmatched strength and unwavering determination. Unlike other heroes who relied on divine weaponry or cunning strategies, Heracles often relied on his raw power and the brute force of his club. The club was more than just a weapon; it was an extension of Heracles himself, a tangible representation of his heroic nature and his ability to overcome seemingly insurmountable challenges. Its simplicity contrasted with the hero's complex character, highlighting his primal power and connection to the natural world. The club was instrumental in defeating many of the monstrous foes he faced, including the Nemean Lion (though he later strangled it), various giants, and other mythical beasts. The club served as a constant reminder of his trials and triumphs, solidifying his status as the greatest hero of Greek mythology.
subtitle: Symbol of Unmatched Strength and Heroism
origin: Self-Made by Heracles, typically from an olive tree.
currentLocation: Lost to history, possibly in the possession of Heracles' descendants or in a forgotten temple.

keyMyths.title: Nemean Lion
keyMyths.description: Heracles initially attempted to kill the Nemean Lion with his club, but its hide was impenetrable. He ultimately strangled the lion instead and used its skin as armor.

keyMyths.title: Geryon's Cattle
keyMyths.description: Heracles used his club to defend himself and his stolen cattle from the Ligurians during his tenth labor, showcasing its effectiveness against mortal opponents.

sources.author: Apollodorus
sources.description: A comprehensive account of Greek myths and legends.
sources.text: A comprehensive account of Greek myths and legends.

sources.author: Diodorus Siculus
sources.description: A universal history that includes accounts of Heracles' labors.
